svg.title: kvm-pilot sensing hierarchy — answer from the cheapest signal first
svg.desc: A three-tier efficiency ladder for sensing target state. Tier one, structured: poll a field or subscribe to an event — events, power and LED state, video signal and resolution, Redfish BootProgress enum, sensors and power and temperatures, SEL and lifecycle logs. Tier two, text: the serial or SOL console as a parseable stream — GRUB, dmesg, kernel panic, getty, installer — when the host redirects to serial. Tier three, pixels, the last resort: local signal, resolution and frame-diff, then OCR, then a vision model, escalating left to right. Colour encodes cost: green means no pixels, amber means cheap on-device pixels, red means a vision model. KVM-capture devices answer in amber and red; BMCs answer in green plus serial text.
svg.text: kvm-pilot — sensing hierarchy
Answer from the cheapest signal the device exposes. Vision is the residual, not the default.
escalate only when the tier above can't answer
1 · Structured — poll a field or subscribe to an event
events — push
power & LED state
video signal + resolution
BootProgress enum
sensors · power · temps
SEL & lifecycle logs
ms-latency JSON or a push subscription — authoritative, no inference, fleet-cheap.
2 · Text — when the host redirects to serial (SOL)
serial / SOL console
GRUB · dmesg · kernel panic · getty · installer
parse a text stream with regex / expect — no OCR, no model.
3 · Pixels — look at the screen (last resort)
signal · res · frame-diff
OCR — Tesseract
vision VLM
Escalate left → right. OCR before a model; the VLM only for graphical or ambiguous screens.
colour = cost · green = no pixels (structured + serial text) · amber = cheap on-device pixels · red = vision model
KVM-capture devices (PiKVM · GLKVM · JetKVM) answer mostly in tier 3; BMCs (Redfish · IPMI) answer in tier 1 + serial text.
